evals.prompt: I'm interested in mechanistic interpretability, scalable oversight, and evaluating agentic LLM systems. Help me turn this into research themes I can use to find PhD advisors, and tell me what evidence would make a professor a strong fit.
evals.expected_output: Research-theme decomposition, fit criteria, evidence checklist, and guidance for distinguishing strong fit from shallow keyword overlap.

evals.prompt: Here are five professors I found for NLP PhD applications. One works on factuality, one on multilingual NLP, one on AI safety evaluations, one on HCI, and one on theory. My projects are in RAG evaluation and LLM-as-judge reliability. Decide which are strongest fit and what I should verify before applying.
evals.expected_output: A faculty-fit comparison table with strong/medium/weak/unknown classifications, evidence, risks, and follow-up checks.

evals.prompt: Build a program-fit table for my PhD shortlist. I care about research fit more than ranking, but I also want to avoid single-advisor risk. My interests are robust evaluation for LLM agents and data-centric ML.
evals.expected_output: A program-fit mapping framework with shortlist criteria, advisor-depth risk assessment, and school-specific notes structure.
